Site Plan application to propose a 6-storey (19.65 m) residential building consisting of 4 two-storey grade-related townhouse units and 67 apartment units, for a total of 71 units. A total of 5,931 square metres of residential GFA is proposed, equating to a FSI of 2.56 times the lot area. The revised applications propose a 232 square metre on-site parkland dedication along the western limit of the site. Site access is proposed via a 7.2 metre wide driveway off Hendon Ave which leads to the loading area and ramp to the UG garage. A total of 44 parking spaces are proposed within one level of UG parking.

Zoning By-law Amendment application for the lands at 1-70 Eglinton Square, 1431 & 1437 Victoria Park Ave, 14, 18, 22 and 26 Engelhart Cres within the Golden Mile Secondary Plan Area to permit a mixed-use development. The application proposes six (6) tall buildings with building heights that range from 20 to 50 storeys and a six-storey mid-rise building. A total gross floor area of 318,654 sq. m (315,845 sq. m for residential uses and 2,809 sq. m for retail uses), 3,618 residential units and an FSI of 4.75 times the area of the lands are proposed. The existing three-storey residential buildings at 14, 18, 22 and 26 Engelhart Cres would be retained. The application is related to Official Plan Amendment application 16 230579 ESC 35 OZ (under appeal).

The application proposes to increase the permitted height and density from the approved heights of 35 and 37-storeys as per By-law 1263-2022 (OLT), to 46 and 58-storeys This height increase would result in a total gross floor area of 73,305 m2, comprised of 71,992 m2 of residential GFA and 1,313 m2 of non-residential GFA at-grade. A total of 1,206 residential units are proposed, including 553 units in the west tower and 653 in the east tower. Vehicular and loading access to the development continues to be proposed from the future east-west private laneway on-site, connecting Henning Avenue and Duplex Avenue. Three (3) levels of underground parking are proposed, containing a total of 216 vehicular parking spaces. The revised buildings maintain the setbacks, stepbacks, commercial uses, and heritage retention of the previous approval.

Additionally, the proposed development provides 1,178.27 square metres of indoor amenity space and 1,287.18 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

Zoning By-law Amendment (22 140023 WET 07 OZ) and Site Plan Control (22 140022 WET 07 SA) applications to facilitate an infill development. The applicant is proposing three new towers, measuring 35-storeys, 45-storeys, and 45-storeys. All three tower sit above an eight-storey podium.The proposed development, includes 1,621 new dwelling units, gross floor area of 109,193 square metres resulting in a density of 2.64 floor space index. The proposal would contain a total of 722 parking spaces located within an underground garage.The three existing buildings on the subject site - two apartment buildings, measuring a 14 storeys and 17 storeys and a 2-storey commercial/amenity building - are proposed to be retained.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment applications to permit three new buildings on the lands, comprising a 58-storey mixed-use condominium building with 4,060 square metres of non-residential uses in the lower floors and 741 dwelling units, a 21-storey purpose built rental building with 287 dwelling units with 310 square metres of community space, and a 9-storey purpose built rental building with 81 dwelling units. The proposal also includes 18 replacement rental units, of which the exact location remains to be determined.   A 3,135 square metre parkland conveyance plus a 772 square metre Stratified Public Park Area and enhanced open spaces with a range of programming across the site are proposed.    A new public street, which would extend Rose Avenue through to the northern boundary of the site and a series of private streets across the site are proposed.     The proposal includes modifications to the layout and uses within the existing buildings to create new amenity and non-residential spaces, which would result in the loss of 18 existing rental dwelling units that would be replaced elsewhere within the site as determined though the development review. The remaining 2,251 rental dwelling units are proposed to be retained, along with 1,828 square metres of existing non-residential space. The existing and proposed buildings would result in 3,360 dwelling units with a gross floor area of 231,028 square metres, and 6,198 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, for a total gross floor area of 237,226 square metres across the site.

Site Plan Control Application proposes the development of a 5-storey residential building. The building will provide a total of 62 residential units, being a mix of 2-bedroom and 3-bedrooms. A paved access from Post Road will be provided, leading to 2 levels of below-grade parking, providing a total of 155 parking spaces; as well as 60 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 13,422.34 square metres and a FSI of 1.84. Additionally, the proposed development provides 424.23 square metres of indoor amenity space and 203.00 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

The OPA would also introduce a new Site and Area Specific Policy that mandates a minimum of 3,150 square metres of gross floor area dedicated to activities related to film, television, and digital media production.
